items.title: Inside Shadow-Earth-053: A China-Aligned Cyberespionage Campaign Against Government and Defense Sectors in Asia
items.description: A China-aligned threat group designated SHADOW-EARTH-053 has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure across at least eight countries in South, East, and Southeast Asia, plus one NATO member state, since December 2024. The group exploits unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, particularly the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access a
items.ai_summary: The SHADOW-EARTH-053 threat group has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure in Asia, exploiting unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, specifically the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access. The attack vector involves targeting unpatched Exchange servers, which allows the threat actors to execute arbitrary code and gain control over the system. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, disruption of critical services, and potential data breaches, affecting government agencies, defense sectors, and critical infrastructure. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately apply the latest security patches to their Microsoft Exchange servers, specifically updating to Exchange Server 2013 CU23, Exchange Server 2016 CU22, or Exchange Server 2019 CU11, and configure their systems to use secure communication protocols. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement robust security measures, such as multi-factor authentication and intrusion detection systems, to prevent and detect similar attacks.

items.title: A stealthy RAT burrowing deep into Android devices
items.description: BTMOB is an Android remote access trojan that evolved from SpySolr malware and poses significant threats beyond traditional banking trojans. The malware combines phishing-led delivery with an APK builder interface that enables rapid payload generation without coding skills. Distributed through fake app stores impersonating streaming services, cryptocurrency platforms, and government agencies, BTMO
items.ai_summary: The BTMOB Android remote access trojan is a stealthy malware that has evolved from the SpySolr malware, posing significant threats to Android devices. It combines phishing-led delivery with an APK builder interface, allowing for rapid payload generation without requiring coding skills, and is distributed through fake app stores impersonating streaming services, cryptocurrency platforms, and government agencies. If exploited, this malware can lead to unauthorized access and control of the infected device, potentially resulting in sensitive data theft, financial loss, and other malicious activities. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust phishing detection and prevention measures, such as educating users about the risks of downloading apps from untrusted sources and using anti-malware software that can detect and block suspicious APK files. Additionally, users should only download apps from official app stores and keep their devices and software up-to-date with the latest security patches.

items.title: Reloaded in a modern Remcos RAT Infection
items.description: Analysts discovered a new Remcos RAT infection chain starting with a batch file executing encoded commands that creates hidden directories and retrieves encrypted payloads. Unlike earlier campaigns relying on PowerShell-hosted .NET loaders, this variant incorporates DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based staging for in-memory payload delivery. The infection begins with a phishing email containing
items.ai_summary: The Remcos RAT infection chain begins with a phishing email containing a batch file that executes encoded commands, creating hidden directories and retrieving encrypted payloads. This variant utilizes DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based staging for in-memory payload delivery, differing from earlier campaigns that relied on PowerShell-hosted .NET loaders. The infection can lead to remote access and control of affected systems, with potential real-world impact including data breaches and ransomware attacks. To remediate, defenders should block suspicious batch files and AutoIt executables, and implement monitoring for unusual PowerShell and Windows Command Shell activity. Additionally, defenders can update their security software to detect and block DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based malware, and apply configuration changes to restrict the execution of encoded commands.

items.title: Operation Dragon Weave: Uncovering a China-Linked Campaign Targeting Czech Republic and Taiwan Using Azure Cloud C2
items.description: A sophisticated cyber-espionage campaign attributed to China-linked actors targets officials and citizens in Czech Republic and Taiwan through spearphishing attacks. The operation deploys malicious ZIP archives containing dual infection paths that ultimately deliver AZUREVEIL, an Adaptix C2 agent. The campaign uniquely leverages Microsoft Azure Blob Storage as a dead-drop command-and-control chann
items.ai_summary: The Operation Dragon Weave campaign, attributed to China-linked actors, utilizes spearphishing attacks to target officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan. The attack vector involves malicious ZIP archives containing dual infection paths, which ultimately deliver the AZUREVEIL malware, an Adaptix C2 agent. If exploited, this campaign can lead to data encryption and exfiltration, with real-world impact including compromised sensitive information and potential disruption of government services. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and monitor Azure Blob Storage activity for suspicious behavior. Specifically, defenders should configure their email clients to block ZIP archives from unknown sources and implement a security information and event management (SIEM) system to monitor for T1071 Application Layer Protocol anomalies.

items.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Campaign Hits Red Hat Cloud Services npm Packages
items.description: A supply chain attack compromised multiple @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, executing malicious payloads automatically during installation via preinstall hooks. The attack uses AES-GCM encrypted payloads and obfuscated JavaScript loaders to harvest GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, cloud credentials (AWS, Azure, GCP), Kubernetes and Vault material, SSH keys, Git credentials, and cryptocurren
items.ai_summary: The Mini Shai-Hulud campaign is a supply chain attack that compromised multiple @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, executing malicious payloads during installation via preinstall hooks. The attack uses AES-GCM encrypted payloads and obfuscated JavaScript loaders to harvest sensitive information such as GitHub Actions secrets, npm tokens, cloud credentials, and SSH keys. The affected packages are part of Red Hat Cloud Services, and the attack vector involves the exploitation of the npm package installation process. To remediate this threat, defenders should remove the compromised packages, update their npm packages to the latest version, and rotate any potentially exposed credentials, such as GitHub Actions secrets and cloud credentials. Additionally, defenders should monitor their cloud accounts and GitHub Actions for suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as multi-factor authentication and least privilege access.

    D -->|T1059.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: FSB’s matryoshka #1/3 – Gamaredon’s gifts that keeps unpacking – GammaPhish and GammaWorm
items.description: Gamaredon, a cyberespionage group operated by Russia's FSB, conducts long-term intrusion operations targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure. This analysis documents their 2026 infection chain, which uses HTML smuggling with weaponized xHTML files delivering RAR archives that exploit CVE-2025-8088 to extract HTA files into Windows Startup directories. The chain deploys
items.ai_summary: The Gamaredon group, operated by Russia's FSB, has been conducting long-term intrusion operations targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure entities. Their 2026 infection chain utilizes HTML smuggling with weaponized xHTML files to deliver RAR archives, which exploit a vulnerability to extract HTA files into Windows Startup directories. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the deployment of GammaPhish and GammaWorm, resulting in data encryption and potential ransomware attacks. To remediate, defenders should update their systems to prevent HTML smuggling and ensure that all archives are scanned for malicious content, specifically looking for RAR archives with embedded HTA files. Additionally, defenders should monitor their Windows Startup directories for suspicious activity and implement measures to prevent the execution of unauthorized HTA files.

items.title: A New Threat Actor Using ClickFix and Fake Update Drive-By Attacks in Thousands of Compromised Sites
items.description: A sophisticated threat actor named DriveSurge operates as an Initial Access Broker using a Pay-Per-Install model to deliver malware at scale. The actor compromises thousands of legitimate websites and uses zTDS (Traffic Distribution System) to silently redirect visitors to malicious content. Victims encounter either FakeUpdates campaigns that impersonate browser update prompts for 11 different bro
items.ai_summary: The DriveSurge threat actor utilizes a Pay-Per-Install model to deliver malware at scale, compromising thousands of legitimate websites and leveraging zTDS to silently redirect visitors to malicious content. The attack vector involves drive-by compromise, where victims are redirected to fake browser update prompts, impersonating updates for 11 different browsers. If exploited, this can lead to the installation of malware, potentially resulting in data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate, defenders should implement robust web application firewalls, ensure all software is up-to-date, and utilize browser extensions that block malicious redirects. Additionally, users should be cautious when encountering browser update prompts and verify the authenticity of such requests.

items.title: Malicious npm packages abuse dependency confusion to profile developer environments
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence identified an active supply chain attack involving malicious npm packages that employ dependency confusion techniques. Between May 28-29, 2026, a threat actor using three maintainer aliases published malicious packages across nine organizational scopes that mirror real corporate namespaces. The packages execute obfuscated reconnaissance payloads through npm lifecycle
items.ai_summary: The malicious npm packages exploit dependency confusion to profile developer environments, specifically targeting npm packages with similar names to legitimate corporate namespaces. The attack vector involves publishing malicious packages across nine organizational scopes, executing obfuscated reconnaissance payloads through npm lifecycle hooks. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, with potential real-world impact on organizations that rely on npm packages, such as software development companies. To remediate, defenders should verify the authenticity of npm packages, ensuring they are installed from trusted sources, and monitor for suspicious activity, such as unusual network traffic or system changes. Additionally, developers should implement secure coding practices, including input validation and secure dependency management, to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: Nimbus RAT: How Threat Actors Are Abusing Microsoft Teams and Google Drive to Deploy a Java RAT
items.description: In April 2026, threat actors deployed Nimbus RAT against a legal industry target using Microsoft Teams voice phishing. The attack began with email bombing (282 emails in 90 minutes), followed by a fake IT helpdesk contact via Teams who convinced the victim to grant Quick Assist remote access. Within 20 minutes, a Java-based RAT was deployed that uses Google Drive and Google Sheets for command-and-
items.ai_summary: The Nimbus RAT threat involves a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting the legal industry, where threat actors use Microsoft Teams voice phishing to trick victims into granting remote access via Quick Assist. The attack begins with a barrage of emails, followed by a fake IT helpdesk contact that convinces the victim to grant access, allowing the deployment of a Java-based RAT within 20 minutes. This RAT utilizes Google Drive and Google Sheets for command-and-control, highlighting the abuse of legitimate services for malicious purposes. To remediate, defenders should implement strict access controls for remote access tools like Quick Assist, ensure multi-factor authentication for all users, and conduct regular security awareness training to prevent such social engineering attacks. Additionally, monitoring for unusual activity related to Google Drive and Google Sheets can help in early detection of this threat.

items.title: CVE-2026-9369: A security flaw has been discovered in NousResearch hermes-agent 2026.4.23. Affe...
items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in NousResearch hermes-agent 2026.4.23. Affected is the function _discover_dashboard_plugins of the file hermes_cli/web_server.py of the component CLI web-dashboard Interface. Performing a manipulation of the argument HERMES_ENABLE_PROJECT_PLUGINS results in incorrect comparison. The attack is only possible with local access. The exploit has been released to the
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9369 is a security vulnerability in NousResearch hermes-agent version 2026.4.23, specifically affecting the _discover_dashboard_plugins function in the hermes_cli/web_server.py file of the CLI web-dashboard Interface component. The vulnerability allows for incorrect comparison when the HERMES_ENABLE_PROJECT_PLUGINS argument is manipulated, but it requires local access to exploit. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access or data breaches for users of the hermes-agent, particularly those with local access to the system. To remediate, users should update to a patched version of hermes-agent, ensuring that the HERMES_ENABLE_PROJECT_PLUGINS argument is properly validated to prevent manipulation, and consider implementing additional access controls to limit local access to the system.

items.description: A vulnerability was identified in NousResearch hermes-agent up to 2026.4.16. This impacts the function execute_code of the file tools/code_execution_tool.py of the component Environment Variable Handler. Such manipulation leads to sandbox issue. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure bu
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9368 is a vulnerability in NousResearch hermes-agent up to version 2026.4.16, specifically affecting the execute_code function of the tools/code_execution_tool.py file in the Environment Variable Handler component. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely, leading to sandbox issues and potentially allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to systems and data. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update hermes-agent to a version later than 2026.4.16 and ensure that all dependent components are updated to the latest versions, specifically updating the tools/code_execution_tool.py file to prevent code execution manipulation.

This affects the function detect_dangerous_command of the file tools/approval.py of the component terminal_tool. This manipulation causes os command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacte
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9367 is a vulnerability in NousResearch hermes-agent up to version 5157f5427f1948, affecting the detect_dangerous_command function in tools/approval.py, allowing for os command injection via remote attack. This can lead to unauthorized execution of system commands, potentially resulting in data breaches, lateral movement, or ransomware attacks. Real-world impact includes exploitation by threat actors to gain initial access to systems, with potential targets including research institutions or organizations utilizing the hermes-agent. To remediate, defenders should update hermes-agent to a version later than 5157f5427f1948 and ensure proper input validation and sanitization in the detect_dangerous_command function. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious system command executions and implementing a web application firewall (WAF) can help detect and prevent exploitation.

items.description: A vulnerability was found in NousResearch hermes-agent 2026.4.23. The impacted element is the function _scan_context_content of the file agent/prompt_builder.py. The manipulation results in injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9366 is a vulnerability in NousResearch hermes-agent version 2026.4.23, specifically in the _scan_context_content function of the agent/prompt_builder.py file, allowing for injection attacks. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely, and a public exploit is available, posing a significant risk to affected systems. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and data breaches, affecting organizations using the NousResearch hermes-agent. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update to a patched version of hermes-agent, if available, or apply specific configuration changes to the agent/prompt_builder.py file to prevent injection attacks, such as input validation and sanitization. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and consider implementing a web application firewall to detect and prevent potential exploitation attempts.

items.title: CVE-2026-9365: A vulnerability has been found in Ettercap up to 0.8.3. The affected element is ...
items.description: A vulnerability has been found in Ettercap up to 0.8.3. The affected element is the function FUNC_DECODER of the file src/dissectors/ec_gg.c of the component GG Dissector. The manipulation of the argument gg leads to heap-based buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The complexity of an attack is rather high. The exploitability is described as difficult. The exploit ha
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9365 is a heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the GG Dissector component of Ettercap versions up to 0.8.3, specifically in the FUNC_DECODER function of the src/dissectors/ec_gg.c file. The vulnerability can be exploited remotely with high complexity and difficult exploitability, allowing attackers to potentially execute arbitrary code on the client-side. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and data breaches, particularly affecting organizations that rely on Ettercap for network security monitoring. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Ettercap to version 0.8.4 or later and ensure that all dependent components are updated accordingly. Additionally, implementing robust network segmentation and access controls can help mitigate the impact of a potential exploit.

items.description: A flaw has been found in projectworlds Online Art Gallery Shop 1.0. Impacted is an unknown function of the file /admin/adminHome.php. Executing a manipulation of the argument social_linked can lead to sql injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9364 is a SQL injection vulnerability in projectworlds Online Art Gallery Shop version 1.0, specifically affecting the /admin/adminHome.php file. The vulnerability can be exploited remotely by manipulating the social_linked argument, allowing an attacker to inject malicious SQL code. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, potentially affecting users of the online art gallery. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update to a patched version of the software, if available, and implement input validation and sanitization on the social_linked argument to prevent malicious input. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious database activity and consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent SQL injection attacks.

items.title: Zoom CISO: AI as Security Enabler, Not Role-Replacer
items.description: As Zoom's CISO, Sandra McLeod, discusses the challenges of securing a global communication platform, the promise of AI-driven security workflows, and advice for aspiring cybersecurity leaders.

items.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
items.description: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

items.title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
items.description: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.

items.title: Trump Signs Executive Order That Invites Vetting of Top AI Models for National Security Risks
items.description: The order establishes a framework for the federal government to vet the national security risks of the most advanced AI systems for up to a month before their public release. The post Trump Signs Executive Order That Invites Vetting of Top AI Models for National Security Risks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI-built ransomware toolkit automates EDR evasion, AD discovery
items.description: A threat actor is using an AI-built ransomware attack toolkit that automates Active Directory discovery and helps evade endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions. [...]

items.title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
items.description: China is stealing data from high-value targets via a sneaky, double-layer spear-phishing campaign that includes the Azureveil malware.

items.title: Securing AI Agents Before They Go Rogue Is Next to Impossible
items.description: High-autonomy agents with broad permissions and unfettered access are a recipe for disaster, and enterprises need to act now before they become the next horror story.

items.title: Google June 2026 Android Update Patches 124 Flaws, One Actively Exploited
items.description: Google on Monday released patches for 124 security vulnerabilities impacting its Android operating system for the month of June 2026, including one high-severity flaw in the Framework component that has come under active exploitation. Tracked as CVE-2025-48595 (CVSS score: 8.4), the security flaw has been described as a case of privilege escalation without requiring any user interaction. The

items.title: White House unveils pared-back AI executive order
items.description: The order notes that federal access to the models should be subject to “appropriate confidentiality, cybersecurity, insider-risk, and intellectual-property protection, use, and nondisclosure requirements.”

items.title: Gamaredon Exploits WinRAR to Deliver GammaWorm and GammaSteel Against Ukraine
items.description: The Russian hacking group known as Gamaredon has been attributed to the continued exploitation of a WinRAR vulnerability to deliver multiple malware families aimed at data theft and propagation. Per Sekoia, the activity involves the weaponization of CVE-2025-8088, a path traversal flaw in WinRAR, to launch an HTML Application payload dubbed GammaPhish, which is then used to retrieve an

items.title: Oracle WebLogic CVE-2024-21182 Added to KEV Catalog After Active Exploitation
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a high-severity security flaw impacting Oracle WebLogic Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, CVE-2024-21182 (CVSS score: 7.5), allows an unauthenticated attacker with network access to take control of susceptible servers. It was

items.title: Microsoft Exchange Online outage causes email delays, failures
items.description: Microsoft is working to address a widespread service issue affecting the mail flow pipeline for Exchange Online customers across North America and Germany. [...]

items.title: Two New Reports Offer Competing Explanations for Cybersecurity’s Growing Crisis
items.description: As AI shortens the path from vulnerability disclosure to exploitation, researchers disagree on whether the problem is inadequate security tools or inadequate operational control. The post Two New Reports Offer Competing Explanations for Cybersecurity’s Growing Crisis appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Russia claims foreign spy agencies hacked officials' phones
items.description: In a statement, Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) said it had uncovered what it described as a "large-scale operation" involving malicious software installed on the mobile devices of senior Russian officials.

items.title: Instagram users locked out after Meta AI abused to steal accounts
items.description: Multiple Instagram users had their accounts hijacked after attackers convinced Meta's AI-powered support tools that they were the legitimate owners. [...]

items.title: Exclusive: How One Line of Code Put Billions of Microsoft Android App Downloads at Risk
items.description: A simple development setting bypassed protections designed to prevent unauthorized Android apps from accessing Microsoft account tokens, exposing billions of installations. The post Exclusive: How One Line of Code Put Billions of Microsoft Android App Downloads at Risk appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Android Update Patches Exploited Zero-Day, 123 Other Vulnerabilities
items.description: Google says the Android vulnerability CVE-2025-48595 has been exploited in limited, targeted attacks. The post Android Update Patches Exploited Zero-Day, 123 Other Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Why the browser is now the front line for AI security
items.description: AI-powered attacks and shadow AI adoption are creating new security risks inside the browser. Push Security explains why browser visibility is becoming critical for both threat detection and AI governance. [...]

items.title: Anthropic Expanding Mythos Access to 150 New Organizations
items.description: Only approximately 50 companies have had access to Mythos until now and they have found thousands of vulnerabilities in their products. The post Anthropic Expanding Mythos Access to 150 New Organizations appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Red Hat removes tainted packages after software pipeline compromise
items.description: According to the company’s preliminary analysis, a compromised GitHub account was used to push the malicious code out to customers, hitting 32 packages downloaded roughly 117,000 times a week.

items.title: CISA flags two-year-old Oracle flaw as actively exploited in attacks
items.description: CISA has ordered government agencies to secure their systems against a high-severity Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerability that was patched two years ago and is now actively exploited in attacks. [...]

items.title: The Zero-Knowledge Threat Actor and the End of Responsible Disclosure
items.description: AI can help attackers generate malware, create malicious payloads, bypass simple security checks, and convert vague malicious intent into functional code. The post The Zero-Knowledge Threat Actor and the End of Responsible Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Vulnerability in HP VoIP Phones Enables Enterprise Network Breaches
items.description: A stack-based buffer overflow bug can be exploited for remote code execution on a vulnerable device. The post Critical Vulnerability in HP VoIP Phones Enables Enterprise Network Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
Beyond Assume-Breach: How AI-Native Security Will Reshape Enterprise Defense
items.description: Twenty years after Dark Reading launched, we're looking ahead at what's next for enterprise security. Spoiler: It's hyper-segmented, AI-orchestrated, and way more sophisticated than your dad's firewall.

items.title: AI-Driven Exploitation is Destroying Vulnerability Management. Here’s How to Handle It.
items.description: AI-driven exploitation timelines are rapidly shrinking, and they are not going to stop shrinking. Vulnerabilities are being discovered, reproduced, and weaponized faster than ever in the history of enterprise security. As a result, the window between a vulnerability being disclosed and indiscriminate exploitation observed across the internet is now measured in hours, not days. The industry's

items.title: Oracle WebLogic Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
items.description: The vulnerability is CVE-2024-21182 and it can be exploited without authentication to hack affected WebLogic servers. The post Oracle WebLogic Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Meta AI Hands Over High-Profile Instagram Accounts to Hackers
items.description: Exploiting a confused deputy weakness, the hackers simply asked the chatbot to link the account to a new email address. The post Meta AI Hands Over High-Profile Instagram Accounts to Hackers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: How Leading Organizations Are Turning EDR Into Operational Resilience
items.description: Most organizations now recognize that endpoint protection alone is no longer sufficient. That's why adoption of endpoint detection and response (EDR) has accelerated rapidly in recent years. Organizations understand that modern attacks move faster, evade traditional prevention controls, and require continuous visibility into suspicious activity across the environment. But owning EDR

items.title: Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages
items.description: Hackers published 96 malicious package versions, injected with a credential-stealing worm similar to Mini Shai-Hulud. The post Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Pakistan-Linked SideCopy Targets Afghanistan Finance Ministry with Xeno RAT
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a spear-phishing campaign likely undertaken by the Pakistan-aligned SideCopy group targeting Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance with an open-source remote access trojan called Xeno RAT. "The campaign opens with a spear phishing delivery - a ZIP archive containing a malicious LNK file bearing a carefully crafted Pashto-language filename,"

items.title: New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
items.description: Password manager Dashlane has disclosed that "fewer than" 20 users on the personal subscription plan had their encrypted vaults downloaded following a brute-force attack launched by an unknown party. On May 31, 2026, the company said an "external" threat actor launched a brute-force attack against certain Dashlane user accounts with the aim of breaking two-factor authentication (2FA)

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9954, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Spain arrests suspected hacker for publishing personal data of police, prosecutors and cyber officials
items.description: Police described the incident as a large-scale disclosure of sensitive personal information that posed a threat to both the affected individuals and the institutions they serve. The data was allegedly posted on multiple internet platforms.

items.title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
items.description: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of "strong bilateral cooperation" between the European Commission and Anthropic.

items.title: Inspector general finds NIST mistakes have made vulnerability database ineffective
items.description: NIST’s National Vulnerability Database (NVD) backlog mushroomed from 13,000 unprocessed security vulnerabilities in February 2024 to more than 27,000 by the end of 2025, “undermining the NVD’s utility and public trust," according to an inspector general report.

items.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
items.description: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
items.description: A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a self-propagating worm. "This is effectively a Mini Shai-Hulud campaign: it uses the same core tactics of install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and potential

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
items.description: Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: New Linux Flaw, PAN-OS Exploit, AI-Powered Attacks, OAuth Phishing and More
items.description: Monday hit like a cron job with anger issues. A busted auth path here, a repo-side faceplant there, some "patched-ish" thing already getting chewed on in the wild, and then the usual bonus round: poisoned dev tools, sketchy forum chatter, phishing kits pretending to be productivity, and AI lowering the bar for people who already thought 'curl | sh' had a personality. The vibe is simple: old

items.title: Unidentified RAT pushes NetSupport RAT, (Mon, Jun 1st)
items.description: Introduction

items.title: YARA-X 1.17.0 Release, (Sun, May 31st)
items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.17.0 release brings 5 improvements (several performance improvements) and 1 bugfix.

items.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Cybersecurity) Progress
items.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary package, we asked readers for a cybersecurity-related caption that captures their thoughts about the industry's last two decades.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 29th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9950, (Fri, May 29th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Analysis of a Year of Files Uploaded to DShield Sensors, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Using the data collected over the past year and using Kibana these two ES|QL query to summarize the data, this shows the list of the most uploaded threat to two DShield sensors (local and cloud) over the past year. I have sorted the activity by months that shows the evolution of files uploaded to the sensors each month. The activity peaked during the winter months (Dec 2025 - Feb 2026) and started decreasing in March 2026 for each sensor.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Reconstructing an Akira Ransomware Kill Chain from Perimeter and Endpoint Logs, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Most Akira write-ups focus on the ransom note or the encryption routine. By the time those show up the interesting forensic work is over. The questions that matter to defenders sit earlier. How did they get in. When did they get domain admin. What did they touch before the binary fired. Those answers live in the days before impact. They sit in two log sources that almost never get joined. The perimeter firewall and the Windows event channel.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
